With a new client, the first thing that we deliver to you are three artifacts.
These are your writing guidelines, your audience personas, and the company context.
I share them in the Slack channel, and we encourage you to review them, leave comments, because these artifacts are used heavily in the content generation process.

The more.

So, this is the proposed content structure, and we typically make heavy edits to this content structure, not because the AI doesn't really know what it's doing, but because we do have to guide it to make sure that, like, all of the H2s are connected to the title, that the flow is coherent, that all of the points build on top of it.
And that it's following writing best principles.
We have a lot of really experienced, talented managing editors on our team.

already have traffic.

And I realize we have like only a couple of minutes left, but I did want to, you know, since we have the whole team,

I just wanted to, you know, talk to you a little bit about like your current content strategy, just like right now, what are you kind of doing with content, what are the challenges, what are the experiments that you're maybe carrying out, Justin, I know that you're starting today, so just anything that you guys are planning in the near term.

Man, so a lot of our content, I'll start quick, and Hasan, you can add, a lot of it has been around product launches, launching product getting out there, Jose has been, has a set of contractors that he's working with, like on a bunch of how-to guides and technical content, a lot of that is probably related to like content DevRel would maybe otherwise be writing, Jose, correct me if I'm right or wrong, but it's like, how to build this, how to do this.
You're absolutely right, the next two articles we're publishing are DeepGram Integrations with PyCon and DeepGram Innovations with, um, Kim, or, uh, Uh,
Another LLM, I apologize, I didn't look it up, but yes.
Quick question, so what are you using internally to track content production?
Are you tracking it in Asana or Monday?
Now we're using a combination of Asana and Notion.
Okay.
Notion has our calendar and Asana has like the editing stages, like oh this one's being edited, this one's being drafted, this one's being worked on, and so on.
Notion, meanwhile, just has the publication dates.
Okay, okay.
I think, I'm not sure if I have access to that Notion doc, because it would be helpful so that we avoid like duplicate content production.
Yeah.
We can certainly share access to the Notion product marketing version for you.
Nice.
And I have a question actually, how much of the content that you guys create is going to be like a detailed content piece versus like an AI repeat?
Like an AI repeat?
10% change of a thing, like a massive amount of content based on the top.
Does the question make sense?
Could you provide a little bit more detail?
Yeah.
Like, for example, Voice.ai agents in pharmacy, Voice.ai agents in healthcare, Voice.ai agents in pharmacies in Kansas.
You know, like sometimes like AI, it's programmatic, like content, which is just meant for SEO purposes that sometimes you don't even feature on the website.
You kind of keep it hidden somewhere, but it just covers SEO.
Yeah.
So initially, so our initial strategy is to actually go with the blog content.
I know like with programmatic content, you already have like, I think the AI apps, like the Voice.ai apps that's already programmatic on your website.
We want to go with like the long form blog content because that influences AI search more.
And it gives us more opportunities.
It is to kind of tell LLMs what you do and establish like some like credibility there.
So that's what we're doing.
So we're leaning more into LLMs rather than like Google SEO to begin with.
Yeah, yeah.
I mean, the SEO is kind of like the, like if you're doing SEO well, you're already going to be like you're doing LLM optimization well already.
But there are some things that LLMs kind of favor more.
So we're doing both really.
SEO is still the foundation of everything that we do.
Okay.
Is there any, will there be any aspect of it that's like programmatic content creation?
Like, you know how AI apps was like 4,000 web pages, but not really, the problem with that is it's not really directed towards a developer audience.
Is there any way to do programmatic content like 1,000 web pages for a developer audience?
So we rank like on speech to text and speech to text API in Google.
need video video.
So,
Yeah, that would be, I would have to like talk to the dev team about that.
It's, we would have to find first like the right angle for programmatic, like find the right kind of pages that we want to emulate.
I mean, it's certainly something that we can support.
The one thing that on your website, you do have a lot of content on it.

And I just don't want us to just add like more content to it because it's going to like exhaust, like your crawl budget is, you know, not going to be, you're going to have very little crawl budget left.
So I just want to do that kind of carefully and really think about the approach.
So if programmatic is something that you're interested in, I can, I can talk about it with our dev team and try to figure out an approach.

I think so, because what's happened is the traffic from AI apps has just gone down, down, down, like over time, partially the Google core updates, but partially because it's not relevant to our core audience of developers.
And so we've not been increasing the organic traffic to our site in any substantial way.
So if there's a way to do programmatic that is relevant to developers, I think that would be an unlock for us because we used to rank for speech-to-text, we no longer do.
We used to rank for speech-to-text API, we no longer do because AI apps never really covered that.
So now I want to give like a, I don't know, like some CPR to our speech-to-text and speech-to-text API SEO rankings.
And my thought is, is programmatic developer-oriented content, is that a way to do that?
But what is programmatic for developers, I don't know.
Yeah, yeah, it's a good idea.
I can dig into this a little bit more to see what are some of the opportunities around this.
What kind of CMS are you using?
Is it, I know you're migrating later this year.
Yeah, we're currently using data CMS and we're migrating.
Sanity.
Yes, okay.
I love Sanity.
Yeah.
That's a great experience.
I'm excited.
Yeah, yeah.
Okay, perfect.
Yeah.
So, headless CMS, it's important to know for our developers.
All right.
I will, we will do some research around that as well.
Okay.
We are at time.
Do you have any questions or comments or, you know, anything that, you know, I didn't get to?
Not for me.
I haven't read in the, should we read the artifacts as the next step?
Those three things?
Yes, please.
Yes, I would appreciate that.
Okay.
Yeah.
So, let's all of us do that.
I haven't done it yet.
So, maybe others haven't, but let's all do that.
Okay.
Well, thank you both.
Thanks, everyone.
you so much.
All right.
I'll follow up in Slack.
Thank you.
See ya.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss Deepgram's content strategy and align on priorities for the GrowthX engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=7.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Refined content strategy focusing on 4 clusters: voice AI builders (general + healthcare-specific), enterprise buyers (drive-thru/pharmacy), and developer resources](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=1949.0)
  - [Shift from API-centric to solutions-oriented messaging for enterprise audience](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=1800.0)
  - [Exploring programmatic content creation to boost SEO rankings for key terms like "speech-to-text API"](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2629.0)
  - [Need to review and provide feedback on GrowthX's initial content strategy artifacts](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2734.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy Refinement

  - [Adjusted focus to 4 main clusters:](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=1952.0)
    1.  Voice AI builders (general)
    2.  Healthcare-specific voice AI builders
    3.  Enterprise buyers (drive-thru/pharmacy focus)
    4.  Developer resources
  - [Enterprise messaging to emphasize solutions over APIs](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=1800.0)
  - [Targeting specific verticals: drive-thru (Jack in the Box pilot) and pharmacy (CVS pilot)](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=1607.0)

### Current Content Approach

  - [Recent focus on product launches and technical how-to guides](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2364.0)
  - [Using contractors for some content creation](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2391.0)
  - [Content tracking split between Asana (editing stages) and Notion (publication calendar)](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2412.0)

### SEO and AI Search Optimization

  - [Prioritizing long-form blog content for AI search influence](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2511.0)
  - [Considering programmatic content creation to boost SEO rankings](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2556.0)
  - [Challenge: AI apps content (4,000 pages) not relevant to core developer audience](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2629.0)
  - [Goal: Improve rankings for "speech-to-text" and "speech-to-text API" keywords](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2646.0)

### Content Production Workflow

  - [GrowthX to conduct keyword research and present initial assignments next week](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2150.0)
  - [Team to review and provide feedback on assignments](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2161.0)
  - [One topic will be selected for calibration to test artifacts and writing guidelines](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2163.0)

### Technical Infrastructure

  - [Currently using Dato CMS, migrating to Sanity (headless CMS) later this year](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2697.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Team to review GrowthX's initial content strategy artifacts (writing guidelines, audience personas, company context)](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2734.0)
  - [GrowthX to explore programmatic content creation opportunities for developer audience](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2581.0)
  - [GrowthX to conduct keyword research and present initial content assignments next week](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2150.0)
  - [Team to provide access to Notion product marketing calendar to avoid duplicate content production](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2442.0)
  - [GrowthX to refine content strategy based on feedback and begin content creation process](https://fathom.video/share/Q8yK6oQ1gPvJ2LBCsqigzb1MKpvcyNsL?tab=summary&timestamp=2718.0)

action_items.description: Update content strategy doc. Combine enterprise & industry-specific clusters. Refocus compliance cluster on voice AI builders (e.g. HeidiHealth, Chartnote)
Begin keyword research for 4 content clusters. Prepare initial assignments for next week's review
Share access to Notion product marketing version w/ Aida
Research programmatic content creation for developer audience w/ dev team. Explore SEO opportunities for speech-to-text, speech-to-text API
Review 3 artifacts shared in Slack (company context, audience personas, writing guidelines)
